products.name: Workday Studio
lab_concepts: Build an EIB Integration from Requirements
recommendation: Simulation is the only viable delivery path because Workday Studio requires a live Workday tenant to execute any meaningful integration workflow — and Workday's proprietary SaaS architecture provides no self-hosted option, no Azure/AWS deployment path, and no programmatic tenant provisioning API. The Eclipse-based IDE can run in a Hyper-V VM, but without a connected tenant, learners cannot build, test, or deploy integrations. Simulation captures the IDE workflow visually and supports AI Vision scoring of screen state, but cannot validate real integration outcomes (XSLT correctness, connector authentication, deployment success). This is the same architectural blocker documented for all Workday products in the Skillable benchmark.

Scoring is limited to AI Vision (evaluating Eclipse IDE screen state — file presence, configuration panel population, error message display) and MCQ knowledge checks (integration pattern selection, XSLT syntax, error handling concepts). Real performance-based scoring — validating that a transformation produces correct output or that a connector successfully authenticates — requires a live Workday tenant and is not achievable on the simulation path. If Workday's product team opens tenant provisioning APIs in the future, script-based scoring via Workday's REST API would become viable and would significantly increase the lab's instructional value.

The blocking question is whether Workday's product team will provide programmatic access to sandbox tenant provisioning for training purposes. The team to engage is Workday's Platform Engineering or Workday Education Product team — not the training leadership, who are willing partners but do not control API access. Verbatim question for your champion to send: 'Does Workday offer a programmatic API to provision isolated sandbox tenants for training and certification purposes? If so, what is the provisioning time, cost model per tenant, and any rate limits on concurrent tenant creation?' Until this question is answered affirmatively, lab investment is not justified.

Workday Education is a willing and capable training partner — mature certification program, Talent Development Partners network, multi-track catalog, and clear demand for Studio training. The blocker is entirely on the product side: Workday's API posture for tenant provisioning. The Skillable benchmark documents over a year of engagement with Workday training leadership without product team API commitment. Maintain the relationship at low cost (no solution engineering investment), revisit if Workday announces a developer sandbox or training tenant program, and do not build lab content until tenant access is confirmed.

If the tenant blocker is resolved, the highest-priority program is Workday Pro certification preparation — specifically for integration developers and platform administrators. The Talent Development Partners (ASU, Marymount) are the natural delivery channel. A secondary program serving Workday implementation partner SEs (presales and delivery engineers who need to demo and configure Studio for customers) would follow the Commvault expansion arc: SE enablement first, then customer training, then partner certification. Workday Rising (annual flagship conference) is a natural events consumption motion for Studio hands-on sessions.

evidence.claim: **Integration Lifecycle Depth | Strength:** Workday Studio covers a multi-phase integration development lifecycle — design (selecting integration pattern: EIB, Core Connector, or Studio), build (XSLT transformation authoring, web service configuration), test (unit testing within the IDE, tenant-side validation), deploy (packaging and deploying to Workday tenant), and monitor (reviewing integration run logs and error handling). Each phase requires distinct skills and produces distinct artifacts. — Workday Studio documentation; Workday Community integration developer resources. **External System Connectivity | Strength:** Workday Studio's primary purpose is connecting Workday to external systems — payroll processors, benefits providers, HRIS platforms, ERP systems, and custom endpoints via SOAP/REST web services. **Integration Debugging Scenarios | Strength:** Debugging failed Workday integrations — tracing XSLT transformation errors, diagnosing web service authentication failures, interpreting integration run logs — is a documented pain point in the Workday Community and a primary driver of Studio training demand. Break/Fix lab scenarios around broken integrations are realistic and high-value. — Workday Community forums; Workday Studio training course descriptions. products.name: Workday Extend
lab_concepts: Build Your First Workday Extend App
Deploy and Monitor an Extend App in Production
recommendation: The only viable lab path for Workday Extend is a pre-provisioned credential pool of Workday developer tenants recycled between learners via Custom API — because Workday runs exclusively on its own SaaS cloud with no VM, container, or Azure/AWS deployment option. This path requires a formal vendor partnership agreement with Workday to establish tenant access at training scale. Without that agreement, no lab can be built. Prioritize securing a developer tenant partnership before investing in lab authoring.

Workday's REST API can validate whether learners correctly created business objects, configured security groups, and deployed Extend apps — enabling partial programmatic scoring for the most critical lab outcomes. UI-driven steps in Workday Studio that are not API-queryable should fall back to AI Vision screen-state assessment. MCQ knowledge checks can supplement for conceptual validation. A hybrid scoring approach (REST API + AI Vision) is the recommended pattern.

The blocking question is: 'Does Workday offer a developer tenant provisioning API or a training-rate tenant program that allows Skillable to spin up isolated sandbox environments per learner at scale?' The team most likely to own this answer is the Workday Developer Relations or Platform Partnerships team — not the training org. Your champion in Workday Education should escalate to their product/platform counterpart. A verbatim question to send: 'We are evaluating Skillable for hands-on Workday Extend training labs. Can Workday provide programmatic tenant provisioning via API, or a pre-provisioned developer tenant pool at training-scale pricing? Who on the platform team owns developer tenant access programs?'

The highest-priority program is Workday Pro Platform Administrator certification prep — because an active certification already exists and creates immediate demand for hands-on practice labs. The second priority is partner/channel enablement for Workday implementation consultants, who need Extend proficiency to differentiate in the ecosystem. Both programs reuse the same lab infrastructure, following the Commvault expansion arc: build once for certification, adapt for partner enablement.

The Workday HCM benchmark documents over a year of engagement with Workday's training leadership that stalled because the product team would not open provisioning APIs. Workday Extend is a different product with a developer-facing API surface, but the same organizational dynamic may apply. Maintain the relationship at low cost and do not invest significant solution engineering time until Workday's platform team commits in writing to developer tenant access for training use cases.

products.name: Workday Integration Cloud
recommendation: Workday Integration Cloud is SaaS-only on Workday's proprietary cloud with no self-hosted, Azure, or AWS deployment path, no per-learner tenant provisioning API, and no NFR/developer sandbox tier — the same fundamental constraints that make Workday HCM a canonical poor match. The only viable real-lab path requires Workday to provide and maintain a shared sandbox tenant with recycled credentials, which demands active vendor cooperation, enterprise licensing investment, and manual cleanup between sessions. Until Workday commits to opening sandbox API access, Simulation is the correct delivery method — AI Vision scoring against a simulated Integration Cloud interface provides a viable but lower-fidelity fallback. Do not invest significant solution engineering time until the Workday product team explicitly commits to sandbox API access.

In a simulation environment, AI Vision scoring evaluates screen state for correct configuration selections, field mappings, and workflow completion steps — sufficient for knowledge validation but not outcome validation. If Workday provides sandbox API access, partial programmatic scoring via RAAS/RaaS REST APIs can verify integration run status and report output, enabling more meaningful performance-based assessment. MCQ knowledge checks are the practical fallback for certification prep scenarios where real tenant access is unavailable.

The blocking question is whether Workday's product team will provide a stable, API-accessible sandbox tenant for lab use at training scale. The team most likely to own this answer is Workday's Platform Engineering or Developer Relations group — not Workday Education, who are willing buyers but do not control tenant provisioning policy. A verbatim question your champion can send: 'Can Workday provide a sandbox Integration Cloud tenant with REST API access (RAAS, WQL) that supports recycled learner credentials, programmatic configuration reset between sessions, and concurrent access for up to 50 simultaneous learners — and if so, what is the licensing and support model for that environment?'

If provisioning is resolved, the highest-value motion is implementation consultant and GSI partner enablement — the hundreds of Deloitte, Accenture, IBM, and boutique Workday partners who need certified integration specialists. This mirrors the Commvault expansion arc: entry through internal SE/presales enablement, then customer training, then partner certification. Workday Pro Integration certification is the natural anchor for a performance-based testing program, and the Talent Development Partners network (ASU, Marymount) provides a ready ATP delivery channel. The business case is strong; the technical gate must open first.

Workday Education is a willing buyer — the training organization is mature, engaged, and has the budget and delivery infrastructure to consume Skillable at scale. The blocker is the Workday product team's API and tenant provisioning policies, which Workday Education does not control. Maintain the relationship with Workday Education at low cost (quarterly check-ins, share relevant case studies from similar ERP/integration vendors), but do not invest significant solution engineering or ProServ resources until the product team commits to sandbox access. This is the canonical 'willing buyer / unwilling product team' pattern documented in the Workday benchmark.
